verses.1: To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
verses.2: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
verses.3: Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
verses.4: Lest my enemy say, I have prevailed against him; and those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
verses.5: But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
verses.6: I will sing to the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.

metadata.source: Webster's Bible (1833) — presented as-is with minor editorial corrections for the ABIDE Bible App.
